title: App & API Protector
subtitle: WAF, DDoS, bot, API security — at the edge

steps.label: The Analogy
steps.body: App & API Protector is the security guard who reads every visitor's ID before letting them into the building. The bad ones never reach your floor.

steps.label: How It Works
steps.body:
AAP is Akamai's unified WAF + DDoS + bot + API security product. Capabilities:

- Kona Rule Set (KRS) — managed WAF rules
- Rate limiting (per IP, per path, per token)
- Adaptive Security Engine — ML-based threat scoring
- API discovery + schema enforcement
- Custom rules in the Property Manager UI

steps.label: Real World
steps.body: Start in alert mode — observe, don't block. Tune false positives over a week or two. Flip to deny mode once your baseline is clean. Audit log lives in Security Center; ship to your SIEM via DataStream.
"AAP blocks bad traffic at the edge — start in alert mode, tune, then enforce."

quiz.question: You just enabled the Kona Rule Set. What mode should you start in?

options.explain: Risk: false positives could block real users on day one.

options.text: Alert mode — observe, tune, then flip to deny
options.explain: Standard practice. Generate alerts for 1–2 weeks, tune false positives, then enforce.
Then why did you enable AAP?

quiz.question: Where do AAP audit logs go for SIEM ingestion?

options.text: Stream via DataStream to your S3 / Object Storage / SIEM
options.explain: DataStream pushes raw security and CDN logs to your destination of choice — Splunk, Elastic, Object Storage, etc.

options.text: Only viewable in the Security Center UI
options.explain: Available in UI and via DataStream.
